title: TRT/SDS Sol/Insol Fractionation for Lysate Prep from Mouse Brains
author: Kasandra Scholz

steps.title: Weigh sample and put in a 2 mL Eppendorf tube
steps.instruction: Weigh sample and put in a 2 mL Eppendorf tube. Keep tubes on dry ice until ready.

steps.title: Add Lysis Buffer (1 ml per...
steps.instruction: Add Lysis Buffer (1 ml per 100 mg) with protease and phosphatase inhibitors, allow the sample to thaw briefly, and homogenize using motorized tissue grinder at a medium speed to avoid excess frothing. Place homogenized samples on ice

steps.title: Use ½ volume of lysis buffer...
steps.instruction: Use ½ volume of lysis buffer to homogenize, transfer to new 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube on ice, add remaining ½ volume of lysis buffer to original tube and homogenize again to rinse off the tissue grinder and move to the Eppendorf tube

steps.title: Rinse the tissue grinder well between...
steps.instruction: Rinse the tissue grinder well between samples by operating in two changes of water, followed by 70% ethanol, and dry with a Kimwipe.

steps.title: Sonicate for 10 seconds on ice...
steps.instruction: Sonicate for 10 seconds on ice (use setting ‘8’ for 10 sec).

steps.title: Set aside 100 µl and mark as “total lysate”
steps.instruction: Set aside 100 µl and mark as “total lysate”.

steps.title: Add Triton X-100 to a final...
steps.instruction: Add Triton X-100 to a final concentration of 1% (10 µl of 10% Tx-100 to 100 µl homogenate).

steps.title: Add SDS to a final concentration of 0
steps.instruction: Add SDS to a final concentration of 0.1% (1 µl of 10% SDS to 100 µl homogenate).

steps.title: Incubate samples for 30 minutes on...
steps.instruction: Incubate samples for 30 minutes on ice, then BCA/Make samples and freeze at -80°C.

steps.title: Add Triton X-100 to a final concentration of 1% (e
steps.instruction: Add Triton X-100 to a final concentration of 1% (e.g., 50 µl of 10% Triton to 500 µl brain homogenate).

steps.title: Incubate samples for 30 minutes on ice
steps.instruction: Incubate samples for 30 minutes on ice.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 15,000 × g for 60 minutes at 4°C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 15,000 × g for 60 minutes at 4°C.

steps.title: Carefully remove supernatant to a fresh tube
steps.instruction: Carefully remove supernatant to a fresh tube. This is the “Triton soluble fraction”.

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in Lysis Buffer...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in Lysis Buffer + 2% SDS (200 µl per pellet) with protease and phosphatase inhibitors and sonicate for 10 seconds Note: SDS will precipitate out of Lysis Buffer when cold; gently warming for a few seconds may be necessary to re-dissolve.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 15,000 × g for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 15,000 × g for 10 minutes and carefully remove supernatant to a fresh tube. This is the “SDS soluble fraction”.

steps.title: STOP here and save pellet at...
steps.instruction: STOP here and save pellet at -80°C for formic acid fractionation.

steps.title: Determine protein concentration using BCA assay
steps.instruction: Determine protein concentration using BCA assay.

steps.title: Aliquot each fraction into several tubes...
steps.instruction: Aliquot each fraction into several tubes to minimize freeze‑thawing and store at -80°C.
